title: Mission Control by Nyk from Builderz
author: Apprehensive-Cow2276
selftext:
Hey everyone,  
  
Like many of you building with AI agents (Claude, OpenClaw gateways, etc.), I was drowning in chaos: agents dying when I went offline, token costs sneaking up without visibility, no clean way to track tasks/pipelines across multiple agents, and GitHub issues scattered everywhere.After grinding on Solana infra and agentic setups for a while, I decided to build something that actually fixes this for real builders  not another cloud-locked SaaS. 

Meet Mission Control: an open-source, fully local-first dashboard for AI agent orchestration.

* Key stuff it does (all running on your machine with SQLite only, zero external deps):
* Real-time panels for tasks, logs, token/cost tracking, pipelines, and agent status
* GitHub sync: turns issues into Kanban boards automatically
* Native Claude session tracking (costs, messages, etc.)
* Works completely offline  no cloud dependency, no "phone home"
* Multi-gateway ready (e.g. OpenClaw integrations coming soon)
* Clean UI for monitoring fleets without 50 browser tabs 

Repo: [https://github.com/builderz-labs/mission-control](https://github.com/builderz-labs/mission-control) (Already at \~1.9k stars in a short time)

To keep the dev grind sustainable, there's a small community token on Solana ([$MCTRL]()) where all creator fees and dev supply go directly to the builder nyk\_builderz. Basically a way for users who love the tool to support ongoing development without VCs or presales. 

CA: GPp2v7X2YgSCCCUCG24id7Td2TTTEeJnF1gY3mKGpump

Not here to shill the token. The tool is free and open-source forever. But if you're using or testing agent fleets and want better visibility (especially offline/local), I'd love your honest feedback: 

* What’s your current biggest pain with agent management?
* Any features you'd kill for (e.g., better alerts, webhook support, more integrations) 
* Screenshots of your setups welcome! 

There's also a small X/Twitter community around the builder if you want to chat live: [https://x.com/i/communities/2030020675696742424](https://x.com/i/communities/2030020675696742424)

Thanks for reading. Excited to hear what you think and iterate based on real user input. Let's make agent orchestration less painful.

comments.author: dogazine4570
comments.body:
This sounds interesting, especially the “not another cloud‑locked SaaS” angle. A lot of us building agent workflows hit the same wall once things move beyond toy demos — visibility and orchestration become the real bottlenecks, not model quality.

A couple questions that would help evaluate it:

- How are you handling agent lifecycle management? Is it process-level supervision (like a local orchestrator) or something container-based?
- Do you support cost attribution per agent/task, or just overall token tracking?
- How opinionated is the pipeline structure? Can it plug into existing setups (e.g., LangChain/LangGraph/custom scripts), or does it require adopting your framework?
- Any built-in retry/backoff or failure recovery logic for long-running tasks?

Also curious how you’re thinking about multi-agent coordination — is it more of a monitoring layer, or does it actively schedule/route tasks between agents?

If you’ve got a repo or architecture overview, would love to see how you’re structuring it. The pain you’re describing is very real — especially once you’re juggling GitHub issues + async agents + infra costs.
